---
title: "Pathway.Significance.Testing"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

# Context

The goal of this quarto document is to delve deeper into the female visitation "pathways" previously explored in other documents. We are interested in these graphs specifically:\
\
![](images/pathways.spawn.success.png)\

![](images/pathways.total.spawns.png){width="698"}\
The code for these graphs is located in Data_Analysis_Code.qmd. These graphs are interesting because not much research has been done on understanding female mate choice, especially in the wrasse. Thus these graphs give us potential insight into the complexity (possibly) of female decision making.

## Code Wrangling

Lets bring the code in.

```{r}
#Install and library important packages
#install.packages('ggplot2')
#install.packages('ggeffects')
#install.packages('RColorBrewer')
#install.packages('here')
#install.packages('MASS')
#install.packages('tidyverse')
#install.packages('janitor')
#install.packages('readxl')
#install.packages('brms')
#install.packages('ProbBayes')
library(ProbBayes)
library(ggeffects)
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(brms)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(tibble)
library(MASS)

#Download the Data
behavior_sheet <- read_excel("UW_Nest_Behavioral_Sheets_Cleaned.xlsx")

#Separate the data into something more digestable
behavior_sheet_date <- as.Date(behavior_sheet$Date)
behavior_sheet$Date <- behavior_sheet_date
behavior_sheet_sep <- behavior_sheet %>%
  mutate(Year = year(Date)) %>%
  mutate(Month = month(Date)) %>%
  mutate(Day = day(Date)) %>%
  relocate(Year, .after = Date) %>%
  relocate(Month, .after = Year) %>%
  relocate(Day, .after = Month)
head(behavior_sheet_sep)

#Fix SAT submission to NM from chr to num
sat_sub <- as.numeric(behavior_sheet_sep$`SAT submission to NM`) #Creates a vector with the same entries but as numbers
behavior_sheet_sep$`SAT submission to NM` <- sat_sub #put it back into the tibble
#Do the same for SNK Submission to SAT
snk_sub <- as.numeric(behavior_sheet_sep$`SNK submission to SAT`)
behavior_sheet_sep$`SNK submission to SAT` <- snk_sub
#Same for NM aggression to SAT
NM_aggsat <- as.numeric(behavior_sheet_sep$`NM aggression to SAT`)
behavior_sheet_sep$`NM aggression to SAT` <- NM_aggsat

behavior_sheet_cln <- behavior_sheet_sep |>
  #Uses the Janitor package to standardize the names
  clean_names()

#Turn the year variable into a character for ease of comparison later on
year_date <- as.character(behavior_sheet_cln$year) #Creates a vector with years as chr
behavior_sheet_cln$year <- year_date #creates a new variable for ease of graphing later on
```

## Different Pathways success rate and total spawns

Let's now create the variables that we are interested in.

```{r}
sig.pathway.dif <- behavior_sheet_cln |>
    #Remove nests where there are data inconsistencies
  filter_out((female_spawning_with_nm_brought_by_nm + female_spawning_with_only_sat_brought_by_nm)/(total_female_visits_nm_brought) > 1) |>
  filter_out((female_spawning_sat_brought + females_spawing_sat_brought_sat_only_spawn)/(female_visit_by_sat) > 1) |>
  filter_out((female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm + female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm_but_only_sat_spawn)/(female_visits_by_sat_passed_to_nm) > 1) |>
  filter_out((female_spawning_snk_brought + female_spawning_snk_brought_skn_only_spawn)/(female_visit_by_snk) > 1)|>
  #Create the success rate variable for each of the pathways
  mutate(
    'nm.fspawn.sucessrate' = (female_spawning_with_nm_brought_by_nm + female_spawning_with_only_sat_brought_by_nm)/(total_female_visits_nm_brought),
    'sat.fspawn.sucessrate' = (female_spawning_sat_brought + females_spawing_sat_brought_sat_only_spawn)/(female_visit_by_sat),
    'satpass.fspawn.sucessrate' = (female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm + female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm_but_only_sat_spawn)/(female_visits_by_sat_passed_to_nm),
    'snk.spawn.sucessrate' = (female_spawning_snk_brought + female_spawning_snk_brought_skn_only_spawn)/(female_visit_by_snk)
    ) |>
  #Go through the columns we just made and swap NAs for 0, since zeros do matter for us
  mutate(
    across(c(nm.fspawn.sucessrate:snk.spawn.sucessrate), ~replace_na(.x,0))
  ) |>
   #Remove nests where there are data inconsistencies
  filter_out((female_spawning_with_nm_brought_by_nm + female_spawning_with_only_sat_brought_by_nm) > (total_spawns_nm_brought + total_spawns_nm_brought_sat_only_spawn)) |>
  filter_out((female_spawning_sat_brought + females_spawing_sat_brought_sat_only_spawn) > (total_spawns_sat_brought + total_spawns_sat_brought_sat_only_spawn)) |>
  filter_out((female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm + female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm_but_only_sat_spawn) > (total_spawns_sat_passed_to_nm + total_spawns_sat_passed_to_nm_but_sat_only_spawn))|>
  filter_out((female_spawning_snk_brought + female_spawning_snk_brought_skn_only_spawn) > (total_spawns_snk_brought + total_spawns_snk_brought_snk_only_spawn))|>
  #Create the number of times female spawned variable for each of the pathways
  mutate(
    'nm.tfspawn' = (total_spawns_nm_brought + total_spawns_nm_brought_sat_only_spawn)/(female_spawning_with_nm_brought_by_nm + female_spawning_with_only_sat_brought_by_nm),
    'sat.tfspawn' = (total_spawns_sat_brought + total_spawns_sat_brought_sat_only_spawn)/(female_spawning_sat_brought + females_spawing_sat_brought_sat_only_spawn),
    'satpass.tfspawn' = (total_spawns_sat_passed_to_nm + total_spawns_sat_passed_to_nm_but_sat_only_spawn)/(female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm + female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm_but_only_sat_spawn),
    'snk.tfspawn' = (total_spawns_snk_brought + total_spawns_snk_brought_snk_only_spawn)/(female_spawning_snk_brought + female_spawning_snk_brought_skn_only_spawn)
  ) |>
    #Go through the columns we just made and swap NAs for 0, since zeros do matter for us
  mutate(
    across(c(nm.tfspawn:snk.tfspawn), ~replace_na(.x,0)))
```

Let's graph these to see if they make sense

Spawning success as a function of total female visits when brought by the nesting male

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(total_female_visits_nm_brought, nm.fspawn.sucessrate)) +
  geom_point() + #Sets the type of graph
  geom_smooth() + #Adds a trendline
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Spawning success when Nesting Male brings the female',
        x = 'Number of females brought by NM',
       y = 'Spawning success rate') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

Spawning success as a function of total female visits when brought by the satellite

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(female_visit_by_sat, sat.fspawn.sucessrate)) +
  geom_point() + #Sets the type of graph
  geom_smooth() + #Adds a trendline
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Spawning success when the Satellite Male brings the female',
        x = 'Number of females brought by SAT',
       y = 'Spawning success rate') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

Spawning success as a function of total female visits when brought by the sat and passed to the nesting male

```{r, warning=FALSE}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(female_visits_by_sat_passed_to_nm, satpass.fspawn.sucessrate)) +
  geom_point() + #Sets the type of graph
  geom_smooth() + #Adds a trendline
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Spawning success when Satellite Male passes the female',
        x = 'Number of females brought by SAT then Passed',
       y = 'Spawning success rate') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

Spawning success as a function of total female visits when brought by a sneaker

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(female_visit_by_snk, snk.spawn.sucessrate)) +
  geom_point() + #Sets the type of graph
  geom_smooth( method = 'lm') + #Adds a trendline
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Spawning success when a Sneaker male brings a female',
        x = 'Number of females brought by a Sneaker',
       y = 'Spawning success rate') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

An average of the total number of times a female spawns when brought by the nesting male

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(x = (female_spawning_with_nm_brought_by_nm + female_spawning_with_only_sat_brought_by_nm), y = nm.tfspawn)) +
  geom_point() + #Sets the type of graph
  geom_smooth( method = 'lm') + #Adds a trendline
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Average number of times a female spawns when brought by the Nesting Male',
        x = 'Number of females that spawned when brought by a Nesting Male',
       y = 'Amount of times a female spawned') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

An average of the total number of times a female spawns when brought by the satellite male

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(x = (female_spawning_sat_brought + females_spawing_sat_brought_sat_only_spawn), y = sat.tfspawn)) +
  geom_point() + #Sets the type of graph
  geom_smooth( method = 'lm') + #Adds a trendline
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Average number of times a female spawns when brought by the Satellite Male',
        x = 'Number of females that spawned when brought by a Satellite Male',
       y = 'Amount of times a female spawned') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

Total amount of spawns per female when brought by the satellite and passed to the nesting male

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(x = (female_spawning_sat_brought + females_spawing_sat_brought_sat_only_spawn), y = satpass.tfspawn)) +
  geom_point() + #Sets the type of graph
  geom_smooth( method = 'lm') + #Adds a trendline
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Average number of times a female spawns when passed by the Satellite Male',
        x = 'Number of females that spawned when passed by a Satellite Male',
       y = 'Amount of times a female spawned') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

Total amount of spawns per female when brought by the sneaker

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(x = (female_spawning_snk_brought + female_spawning_snk_brought_skn_only_spawn), y = snk.tfspawn)) +
  geom_point() + #Sets the type of graph
  geom_smooth( method = 'lm') + #Adds a trendline
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Average number of times a female spawns when brought by the Sneaker Male',
        x = 'Number of females that spawned when brought by a Sneaker Male',
       y = 'Amount of times a female spawned') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

Now we are going to run some significance testing between the pathways for spawning success and total number of female spawns, so let's get the tibbles we are going to use

```{r}
#We are going to move the columns (our variables) under 1 column for easier analysis
sig.pathway.dif.tspawn.selected <- sig.pathway.dif |>
  #select only for the columns we want
  dplyr::select(nm.tfspawn:snk.tfspawn) |>
  #Pivot the colomns into rows
  pivot_longer(nm.tfspawn:snk.tfspawn,
               names_to = 'pathway.total',
               values_to = 'total.spawns')

sig.pathway.dif.successrate.selected <- sig.pathway.dif |>
   #select only for the columns we want
  dplyr::select(nm.fspawn.sucessrate:snk.spawn.sucessrate) |>
   #Pivot the colomns into rows
  pivot_longer(nm.fspawn.sucessrate:snk.spawn.sucessrate,
               names_to = 'pathway.successrate',
               values_to = 'successrate')
```

Let's check the shape of the pathway distributions, this will inform us with what tests and distributions we are going to run.

Nesting Male Success rate distribution

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(sig.pathway.dif$nm.fspawn.sucessrate)) +
  geom_histogram() + #Sets the type of graph
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Nesting Male success rate distribution',
        x = 'Nesting male success rate',
       y = 'Count') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
  
```

Satellite Male success rate

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(sig.pathway.dif$sat.fspawn.sucessrate)) +
  geom_histogram() + #Sets the type of graph
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Satellite Male success rate distribution',
        x = 'Satellite male success rate',
       y = 'Count') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

Satellite Male passing success rate

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(sig.pathway.dif$satpass.fspawn.sucessrate)) +
  geom_histogram() + #Sets the type of graph
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Satellite Male Passing success rate distribution',
        x = 'Satellite male passing success rate',
       y = 'Count') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

Sneaker success rate

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(sig.pathway.dif$snk.spawn.sucessrate)) +
  geom_histogram() + #Sets the type of graph
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Sneaker Male success rate distribution',
        x = 'Sneaker male passing success rate',
       y = 'Count') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

Nesting Male total spawns distribution

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(sig.pathway.dif$nm.tfspawn)) +
  geom_histogram() + #Sets the type of graph
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Nesting Male total spawns distribution',
        x = 'Nesting male total spawns',
       y = 'Count') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
  
```

Satellite Male total spawns distribution

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(sig.pathway.dif$sat.tfspawn)) +
  geom_histogram() + #Sets the type of graph
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Satellite Male total spawns distribution',
        x = 'Satellite male total spawns',
       y = 'Count') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

Satellite male passing total spawns

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(sig.pathway.dif$satpass.tfspawn)) +
  geom_histogram() + #Sets the type of graph
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Satellite Male passes total spawns distribution',
        x = 'Satellite male passes total spawns',
       y = 'Count') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

Sneaker male total spawns

```{r}
ggplot(sig.pathway.dif, aes(sig.pathway.dif$snk.tfspawn)) +
  geom_histogram() + #Sets the type of graph
  theme_classic() + #Sets the plot visuals
  labs(title = 'Sneaker Male total spawns distribution',
        x = 'Sneaker male total spawns',
       y = 'Count') + # Labels the axes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) #Moves title to the middle
```

\
\
\

Now let's run some Kruskal-Wallis tests (we run this type because our data is not normally distributed thus ANOVAs wouldn't fit. It uses sample medians instead of sample means to compare groups like an ANOVA does). Before, let's get some descriptive statistics

```{r}
group_by(sig.pathway.dif.successrate.selected, pathway.successrate) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    mean = mean(successrate, na.rm = FALSE),
    sd = sd(successrate, na.rm = FALSE),
    median = median(successrate, na.rm = FALSE),
    IQR = IQR(successrate, na.rm = FALSE)
  )

group_by(sig.pathway.dif.tspawn.selected, pathway.total) %>%
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    mean = mean(total.spawns, na.rm = FALSE),
    sd = sd(total.spawns, na.rm = FALSE),
    median = median(total.spawns, na.rm = FALSE),
    IQR = IQR(total.spawns, na.rm = FALSE)
  )
```

Now let's run the test

```{r}
#Run the Kruskal test
kruskal.test(successrate ~ pathway.successrate, data = sig.pathway.dif.successrate.selected)
#Now run a Wilcoxon rank sum test to see differences between pathways
pairwise.wilcox.test(sig.pathway.dif.successrate.selected$successrate, sig.pathway.dif.successrate.selected$pathway.successrate, p.adjust.method = 'BH')
```

```{r}
#Run the Kruskal test
kruskal.test(total.spawns ~ pathway.total, data = sig.pathway.dif.tspawn.selected)
#Now run a Wilcoxon rank sum test to see differences between pathways
pairwise.wilcox.test(sig.pathway.dif.tspawn.selected$total.spawns, sig.pathway.dif.tspawn.selected$pathway.total, p.adjust.method = 'BH')
```

What this tells us is that the sat, sat passing, and sneaker pathways are significantly different from the nm pathway. Neither sat pathway is significant from the other. And the sneaker path is significantly different from all of them. While this is joyous results, it is very helpful for us and will allow us to think about our data in new ways.

## Brms modeling

While our previous work tells us that the pathways are not significantly different (other than the nesting male pathway), we are still interested in the pathways and can build some models looking at our data! For this section, we will be using Bayesian statistics, rather than frequentest (purely because I was taught R modeling with Bayesian stats).

First, let's look to see if there is a relationship between the pathway that a female visits and the chance of her spawning. For this, we are going to run 2 different models. First, we will simply count the number of females that spawn in each pathway and run a negative binomial model. This should tell us the how the number of females that spawn in each pathway increases with each 1 visit of each pathway.

```{r}
path.fspawning.nb <- behavior_sheet_cln |>
    #Remove nests where there are data inconsistencies
  filter_out((female_spawning_with_nm_brought_by_nm + female_spawning_with_only_sat_brought_by_nm)/(total_female_spawning) > 1 ) |>
  filter_out((female_spawning_sat_brought + females_spawing_sat_brought_sat_only_spawn)/(total_female_spawning) > 1) |>
  filter_out((female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm + female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm_but_only_sat_spawn)/(female_visits_by_sat_passed_to_nm) > 1) |>
  filter_out((female_spawning_snk_brought + female_spawning_snk_brought_skn_only_spawn)/(total_female_spawning) > 1)|>
  #Create new variables that account for the pathway's successes
  mutate(
    'nm.path' = (female_spawning_with_nm_brought_by_nm + female_spawning_with_only_sat_brought_by_nm),
    'sat.path' = (female_spawning_sat_brought + females_spawing_sat_brought_sat_only_spawn),
    'sat.pass.path' = (female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm + female_spawning_sat_passed_to_nm_but_only_sat_spawn),
    'snk.path' = (female_spawning_snk_brought + female_spawning_snk_brought_skn_only_spawn)
  ) |>
  #Move the pathways columns we just created into their own column for inputting into the model
  pivot_longer(cols = c(nm.path:snk.path),
               names_to = 'Pathway',
               values_to = 'number.spawned')

path.fspawning.nb.mdl1 <- brm(data = path.fspawning.nb,
      family = negbinomial(link = 'log'), # Sets the models distribution
      number.spawned ~ 0 + `Pathway` + (1 | nest_name), # Gives the model the formula
      iter = 4000, warmup = 1000, chains = 4, cores = 4, #Markov chain parameters
      seed = 4, #Determines which random numbers
    sample_prior = 'yes', #Allows us to run hypothesis tests of the different Pathways
      file = 'Exploratory model outputs/path.fspawning.nb.mdl1') #saves it as an output

#Gives a plot of the model's predictions
pp_check(path.fspawning.nb.mdl1)
#Gives us the summary of the model
print(path.fspawning.nb.mdl1, digits = 4)
#Plots the Markov chains
plot(path.fspawning.nb.mdl1)
```

Then, we will run a model with the response variable being the probability of a female spawning for each individual pathway using a binomial model, which will tell us the probability of a female spawning based on our data.

```{r}
pathway.fspawns.mdl.b <- brm( data = path.fspawning.nb,
    family = binomial(link = 'logit'), #Tell model what type of distribution
    #Specifying the model
    formula = bf(number.spawned | trials(total_female_visits) ~ 0 + Pathway + (1|nest_name)),
    iter = 4000, warmup = 1000, chains = 4, cores = 4, #Markov chain parameters
      seed = 4, #Determines which random numbers
    sample_prior = 'yes', #Allows us to run hypothesis tests of the different Pathways
      file = 'Exploratory model outputs/pathway.fspawns.mdl.b') #saves it as an output

#Gives a plot of the model's predictions
pp_check(pathway.fspawns.mdl.b)
#Gives us the summary of the model
print(pathway.fspawns.mdl.b, digits = 4)
#Plots the Markov chains
plot(pathway.fspawns.mdl.b)
```
